items.headline: Study session on zoning and parkland protections
items.summary: City Council will review existing zoning designations, make corrections, and consider state law protections for recreational facilities and parkland sites. This affects how Cupertino's parks and recreational areas are legally designated and protected.

items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Building permits issued for two 2-story single-family homes at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Dr, totaling about $1.1 million in construction value. The paired development adds housing units to the neighborhood.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank opens new Palo Alto branch on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The bank received permits to install illuminated signage at 250 Lytton Avenue, indicating a new branch location in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: Sunnyvale to study eliminating chemical pesticides on city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study a policy to stop using chemical pesticides on all city-owned and leased land. This would affect maintenance of parks, medians, and other municipal grounds.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission to review artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study city policy on artificial turf, likely examining environmental and health impacts of synthetic surfaces used in parks and recreational areas.
